title: Conditionals
description: Use zero, first, second, and third conditionals.

questions.question: If it rains, we ___ at home.
If I had known, I ___ you.
Which is a second conditional sentence?
options: If I win, I will celebrate.
If I were rich, I would travel.
If he had left early, he would have arrived on time.
questions.answer: If I were rich, I would travel.

questions.question: Complete: If she studies, she ___ pass.

title: Reported Speech
description: Convert direct speech to reported speech.

questions.question: Direct: He said, 'I am tired.' Reported:
options: He said that he is tired.
He said that he was tired.
questions.answer: He said that he was tired.

questions.question: Direct: She said, 'I can swim.' Reported:
Which reporting verb is correct? 'Come here,' he ___ me.
Time expression usually changes from 'today' to:

title: Academic Vocabulary
description: Understand advanced words in formal contexts.

questions.question: The results were ___ with the original hypothesis.
The author used data to ___ the argument.
